Empires & Puzzles Queen Guowang Review: The Vengeful Monarch

Queen Guowang forces a difficult choice: let her team gain mana every time you fire a special, or dispel her and take massive damage. She is the queen of 'damned if you do, damned if you don't.' But does her Average speed hold her back in today's Fast meta?
Queen Guowang: Punishment for Playing the Game
Queen Guowang, The Radiant Ruler: Punishing Dispel with Style

Queen Guowang is a 5-star Legendary Holy hero from the Journey family, released in early 2023. She is a Sorcerer who redefined the Holy tank role for a period, bringing a potent mix of AoE damage, team-wide mana acceleration, and a unique "Vengeance" mechanic that punishes enemies for trying to counter her. She is elegance and lethality combined.

The Queen is depicted in regal, flowing robes with a commanding presence. Her art is intricate and beautiful, fitting her title as the "Unparalleled Beauty" (her fancy name). She looks every bit the part of a ruler of the "Kingdom of Women" from
, exuding calm authority while chaos erupts around her.
In late 2025, Queen Guowang's stats are showing their age, similar to her Journey peers.
Low by modern standards. She relies on her high skill multiplier (330%) to deal damage, but she isn't a stat-stick nuker.
These were tank-worthy stats in 2023 but are now flank-level or offensive-only stats. She is no longer the impenetrable wall she once was.
This skill is a tactical nightmare for the opponent:
330% damage to all enemies. At Average speed, this is a very strong hit. It clears minions and softens the entire enemy team significantly.

"All allies get +10% mana every time an enemy uses their Special Skill." This is her most unique feature. It turns the enemy's offense into your defense. If they fire at you, they charge your team to fire back.
If the Vengeance buff is dispelled by an enemy Special Skill, all enemies take
damage. This serves as protection for her Mana buff. The enemy
to dispel the mana generation, but doing so triggers a massive damage bomb.
The Vengeance damage is fixed (around 525) and does not scale with her attack stat, unlike Erlang Shen's scaling damage. This makes it reliable but capped. Also, it specifically requires an
to dispel it (so tile-based dispels or passives might bypass it depending on the version logic).

. With 10 tiles, she is slower than the Fast meta. However, her passive (Mana on Buff) and her special skill (Mana when hit) create a feedback loop that can make her effectively Fast once she gets going.
If she hits and then gets dispelled, she deals devastating damage to the entire enemy board.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
Extending the duration of her Vengeance and Mana buffs is excellent. A 4-turn mana gen window is scary; 7 turns is a game-winner.
Essential for an Average speed hero. It helps her charge her first shot faster if paired with buffers.
. Highly situational. It helps against specific fiend summoners (like Waterpipe or newer threats) but doesn't universally boost her performance like Attack Up or Defense Up would.
Queen Guowang was a top-tier tank upon release. She brought Yellow tanks back into the meta for a brief period. Her ability to punish dispels was revolutionary.

The damage (330%) is still respectable. However, her mana generation buff was controversial and subject to balance discussions (some versions/modes adjusted it to 6% or modified how it worked). Today, modern "Overpower" heroes hit harder and faster, pushing her out of the Tank role.
. She is still a great hero to bring against "spammy" teams (like those with very fast mana) because they feed her mana generation.
As of this review, Queen Guowang does not have a costume.
She needs to survive to fire. If she dies with 90% mana, she does nothing.
330% to all is a high multiplier. Boosting attack makes her a genuine wipe threat.
. Her utility (Mana Gen + Vengeance) is her main selling point. She needs to live to cast it.
Makes dispellers like Sabina or C.Panther hesitate.
Uniquely charges her team based on enemy actions.
buffs (Hatter, El Naddaha, etc.) do NOT trigger Vengeance. This is her hard counter.
If the enemy just uses tiles and doesn't fire specials, her mana generation buff does nothing.
Can be too slow against Rush teams if she doesn't get tiles.
Forces the enemy to bring specific counters or risk blowing themselves up.
Great against fast, low-damage spam teams.
Good for clearing waves and keeping mana high.
Queen punishes dispel. Sif/Hachiko punish hitting (Counterattack). The enemy has no good options.
Maximum mana generation. Ludwig charges Queen, Queen charges everyone else when enemies fire.
(Sometimes minions dispel without counting as a "Special Skill" depending on coding, though usually it's the summoner's skill).
. If you silence the enemy, they can't fire specials, so your team doesn't gain mana from Queen's buff. (Though preventing enemy specials is usually good anyway).
She is a fantastic pull. She offers damage and unique support. Level her immediately.
She is a strong war option. Be aware of the "Buff Steal" counters. She pairs beautifully with other Journey heroes to create long-lasting status hell.
"Queen Guowang" translates to "Queen King" or "King Queen," which amused many players.
In Beta, she had a Blind effect that was removed before release. Many players lamented this loss, feeling it would have made her the ultimate tank.

Queen Guowang is a hero who changes the rules of engagement. She turns the enemy's strength (using specials) into their weakness (charging your team). While not the invincible monarch she once was, she commands respect on the battlefield.
A- Tier. A powerful and unique hero who demands a specific answer from the opponent.
Long may she reign... and punish those who dare dispel her!

A comprehensive review and strategy guide for Queen Guowang, the Holy element Journey family hero in Empires & Puzzles. Explore her powerful AoE damage, unique mana generation mechanics, and anti-dispel Vengeance.
